title: Integrated tube-trailer and stationary ground storage system and method for enhanced pressure consolidation operations for refueling of gaseous fuels
abstract: An enhanced operating method and an integrated tube-trailers and stationary ground storage system are provided for implementing enhanced pressure consolidation operations for refueling gaseous fuels. The integrated tube-trailers and stationary ground storage system includes gaseous fuel supply tube-trailers and stationary ground storage including at least one or more stationary pressure vessels. A compressor can be supplied by both the stationary ground storage and the tube-trailers. The stationary ground storage can provide higher pressure to the compressor than the tube-trailers, enabling enhanced compressor throughput during refueling operation, and enhanced utilization of tube-trailers payload.
claims: Claims Claims What is claimed is: 1. An integrated tube-trailers and stationary ground storage system for implementing enhanced pressure consolidation operations for refueling gaseous fuels comprising: a gaseous fuel refueling station includes gaseous fuel supply pressure vessels of tube-trailers and stationary ground storage including at least one or more stationary pressure vessels; a compressor; a separate control unit respectively coupled to the tube-trailers and the stationary ground storage of said pressure vessels with each of the control units coupled to said compressor; and said stationary ground storage enabling substantially higher pressure than said gaseous fuel supply pressure vessels of tube-trailers, enabling enhanced compressor throughput during refueling operation. 2. The system as recited in claim 1 wherein said stationary ground storage enabling substantially higher pressure than said gaseous fuel supply pressure vessels of tube-trailers, enabling significantly improved station utilization Â· 3. The system as recited in claim 1 wherein said stationary ground storage enabling substantially higher pressure than said gaseous fuel supply pressure vessels of tube-trailers, enabling effectively and efficiently using one said compressor together with reducing the refueling costs. 4. The system as recited in claim 1 includes said separate control units consolidating gaseous fuel from said gaseous fuel supply pressure vessels of tube- trailers into said stationary ground storage. 5. The system as recited in claim 1 includes said stationary ground storage at the high pressure directly used for refueling. 6. The system as recited in claim 5 includes gaseous fuel directly drawn from a vessel with the highest pressure of the stationary ground storage to fill a vehicleâs tank. 7. The system as recited in claim 1 wherein said compressor includes a single stage compressor. 8. The system as recited in claim 1 wherein said compressor includes a first stage and a second stage and said first stage and said second stage are independently and simultaneously capable of handling a separate gaseous fuel stream capable of handling a separate gaseous fuel stream. 9. The system as recited in claim 1 includes a dispenser coupling a gaseous fuel flow from said stationary ground storage for refueling a vehicle. 10. The system as recited in claim 1 includes a high-pressure buffer storage, and wherein said high-pressure buffer storage is coupled to a dispenser coupling a gaseous fuel flow for refueling a vehicle. 11. The system as recited in claim 1 includes a high-pressure buffer storage, and wherein said high-pressure buffer storage is filled from said stationary ground storage through said compressor. 12. The system as recited in claim 1 wherein said stationary ground storage enabling substantially higher pressure than said gaseous fuel supply pressure vessels of tube-trailers, enabling enhanced payload utilization of the tube-trailers. 13. The system as recited in claim 1 includes a dispenser connected to a vehicle for refueling, and wherein said separate control units implement predefined operational modes responsive to said dispenser being connected to said vehicle. 14. A method for implementing enhanced pressure consolidation operations for refueling gaseous fuels in in an integrated tube-trailers and stationary ground storage system, said method comprising: providing a gaseous fuel refueling station including gaseous fuel supply pressure vessels of tube-trailers and providing stationary ground storage including at least one or more stationary pressure vessels; providing a compressor for consolidating gaseous fuel from said gaseous fuel supply pressure vessels of tube-trailers to said stationary ground storage; providing said compressor for refueling gaseous fuel directly from said stationary ground storage to a vehicle; and providing said stationary ground storage with substantially higher pressure than said gaseous fuel supply pressure vessels of tube-trailers, enabling enhanced compressor throughput during refueling operation and enabling higher utilization of tube-trailers payload. 15. The method as recited in claim 14 includes providing a high-pressure buffer storage for directly fueling vehicle tank; providing separate control units and said compressor for selectively consolidating gaseous fuel from said gaseous fuel supply pressure vessels of tube-trailers to said stationary ground storage, replenishing said high-pressure buffer from said stationary ground storage and refueling a vehicle tank selectively from said stationary ground storage and said high-pressure buffer storage. 16. The method as recited in claim 14 includes providing gaseous fuel pressure of said stationary ground storage to maximize throughput of said compressor during station operation and enhance refueling capacity. 17. The method as recited in claim 14 includes providing gaseous fuel pressure of said stationary ground storage to provide enhanced tube-trailer payload utilization Â· 18. The method as recited in claim 14 includes providing gaseous fuel pressure of said stationary ground storage for enabling effectively and efficiently using one said compressor and reducing the refueling costs 19. The method as recited in claim 14 wherein providing said compressor includes providing a single stage compressor. 20. The method as recited in claim 14 wherein providing said compressor includes providing said compressor with a first stage and a second stage, and both said first stage and said second stage selectively combined and independently capable of handling a separate gaseous fuel stream.
